---
title: "<img src='ARC_Logo.png' style='float: right; width: 250px;'/>**Platelet:RBC Ratio by LTOWB Usage 2018-2025** "
author: "American Red Cross Biomedical Services"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%a %d %B %Y')`"
# output: html_document
always_allow_html: true
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
    # css: style.css
    includes:
      # in_header: "/ARC_Logo.png"
  classoption: landscape
    # mainfont: Arial
  pdf_document: 
    keep_tex: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
  word_document: default
  # github_document: default
  fig_caption: yes
  keep_md: yes

---


<style>
.main-container {
  max-width: 75% !important;
}
/* ---------- Base tab styling ---------- */
.nav-tabs > li > a {
  background-color: #f3f3f3 !important;   /* inactive light */
  color: #444 !important;
  font-weight: 500;
  transition: all 0.25s ease-in-out;
}

/* Remove default borders */
.nav-tabs > li > a {
  border: 1px solid #ddd !important;
}

/* ---------- ACTIVE tabs ---------- */
.nav-tabs > li.active > a {
  color: white !important;
  font-weight: 600;
}

/* CSP%-Up → GREEN */
.nav-tabs > li.active > a[aria-controls="csp-up"] {
  background-color: #2ca02c !important;
  border-color: #2ca02c !important;
}

/* CSP%-Down → RED */
.nav-tabs > li.active > a[aria-controls="csp-down"] {
  background-color: #d62728 !important;
  border-color: #d62728 !important;
}

/* ---------- Icons ---------- */
.nav-tabs > li > a[aria-controls="csp-up"]::before {
  content: "▲ ";
  color: #2ca02c;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.nav-tabs > li > a[aria-controls="csp-down"]::before {
  content: "▼ ";
  color: #d62728;
  font-weight: 700;
}

/* ---------- Smooth fade / slide animation ---------- */
.tab-pane {
  animation: fadeSlide 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

@keyframes fadeSlide {
  from {
    opacity: 0;
    transform: translateY(6px);
  }
  to {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: none;
  }
}
</style>

<style>
/* Low */
.cluster-low ~ .nav-tabs > li.active > a {
  box-shadow: inset 0 -4px 0 #b8c0a8;
}

/* Moderate */
.cluster-moderate ~ .nav-tabs > li.active > a {
  box-shadow: inset 0 -4px 0 #ffbf00;
}

/* High */
.cluster-high ~ .nav-tabs > li.active > a {
  box-shadow: inset 0 -4px 0 #dd5e5e;
}
</style>

<style>
/* Badge look */
.nav-tabs > li > a code {
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25);
  padding: 2px 6px;
  border-radius: 10px;
  font-size: 0.85em;
  margin-left: 4px;
}
</style>
<style>
/* Sticky tabs */
.nav-tabs {
  position: sticky;
  top: 0;
  z-index: 1000;
  background: white;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;
}
</style>

<style>
/* ---------- ACTIVE SUB-TAB TEXT COLOR ---------- */
/* Nested tabsets only */
.tabset .tabset .nav-tabs > li.active > a,
.tabset .tabset .nav-tabs > li.active > a:hover,
.tabset .tabset .nav-tabs > li.active > a:focus {
  color: #000000 !important;   /* black */
  font-weight: 600;
}
</style>



<h2 style="font-size:16px; font-weight:bold;">
Temporal Patterns in Platelet‑to‑Red Cell Transfusion Ratios Across Hospitals With Varying LTOWB Utilization
</h2>
## Analysis{.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}
### LTOWB% by year{.tabset}
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)       # mixed models
library(lmerTest)   # p-values & Type III tests (Satterthwaite/KR)
library(emmeans)    # marginal means & pairwise comparisons
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(data.table)
library(htmltools)
library(downloadthis)
library(writexl)
library(gt)
library(sumExtras)

set.seed(123)
hospdetails <- read_excel('E:\\ARC\\Projects\\Tim\\csp\\hospitals.xlsx') |>
  filter("Master Customer Cd" != "") |>
  select("Master Customer Cd", "Master Customer Nm", "Ship To Addr1","Ship To City","Ship To State","Ship To Zip","Ship To Cntry", "cancer_ctr_flg","trauma_ctr_flg","Bed Cnt","Surgeries Cnt")           
  
df <- read_excel('E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\ltwobhosp.xlsx')
df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(customer_cd), !is.na(pltrbcratio)) 
# Ensure factor ordering (optional)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    ltwobcat = factor(ltwobcat, levels = c("<2","2-5",">5")),
    calendar_yr = as.integer(calendar_yr)
  )
df -> hosp


hosp_pct <- hosp %>%
  group_by(calendar_yr, ltwobcat) %>%
  summarise(n_hosp = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(calendar_yr) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n_hosp),
    pct = n_hosp / total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

theme_jama <- function(base_size = 13){
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(family = "Arial"),     # JAMA uses Helvetica/Arial
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 1),
      legend.position = "top",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),
      axis.title = element_text(size = base_size)
    )
}
jama_gray <- c(
  "<2" = "gray70",
  "2-5" = "gray50",
  ">5" = "gray20"
)

ggplot(hosp_pct,
       aes(x = calendar_yr,
           y = pct,
           group = ltwobcat,
           color = ltwobcat)) +
  
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  
  scale_color_manual(
    values = jama_gray,
    name = "LTWOB category"
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(hosp_pct$calendar_yr))
  ) +
geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(100*pct,1), "%")),
              vjust = -0.8,
              size = 3.3) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Percentage of Hospitals in Each LTWOB Category by Year",
    x = "Calendar Year",
    y = "Percentage of Hospitals"
  ) +  theme_jama()
```


**Percentage of Hospitals in Each LTWOB Category by Calendar Year**
- This figure displays the proportion of hospitals falling into each low‑titer O whole blood (LTOWB) utilization category—<2%, 2.5–5.0%, and >5%—from 2018 through 2025. Percentages represent the share of all hospitals reporting data in a given year.

- **Key Findings**
  + **Growth of higher LTWOB categories over time.**
  + Hospitals utilizing LTWOB at >5% or 2.5–5% remained a minority but expanded steadily after 2022.
- The >5% category increased from 1.4% in 2022 to 18.1% in 2024 and 18.1% in 2025.
- The 2.5–5% category increased modestly, reaching 11.1% in 2024–2025.

**Inflection point in 2022.**
- In 2022, the <2% category temporarily peaked at 97.1%, coinciding with the lowest representation of higher‑use hospitals (<3% in both mid‑tier and high‑tier categories).<br>
- Beginning in 2023, however, the distribution began to shift as more hospitals incorporated LTWOB at higher percentages.

**Emerging adoption pattern after 2023.**
- Between 2023 and 2025, the proportion of hospitals performing >5% LTWOB usage increased more sharply compared with the 2.5–5% group, suggesting movement directly from low (<2%) to high (>5%) uptake rather than gradual progression. 

#### Hospitals increasing LTOWB use{.tabset #csp-up}
##### List
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
hosp <- hosp %>%
  mutate(
    ltwobcat = factor(ltwobcat,
                      levels = c("<2", "2-5", ">5"))
  )

remove(switchers)
switchers <- hosp %>%
  arrange(customer_cd, calendar_yr) %>%
  group_by(customer_cd) %>%
  summarise(
    less_than_2pct  = any(ltwobcat == "<2"),
    greater_than_5pct = any(ltwobcat == ">5"),
    switch_year = ifelse(
      less_than_2pct & greater_than_5pct,
      min(calendar_yr[ltwobcat == ">5"]),  # first year >5%
      NA
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(less_than_2pct & greater_than_5pct)

switchers <- switchers %>% 
  left_join(select(hosp, customer_cd, master_customer_nm), by = "customer_cd") %>%
  select(customer_cd, master_customer_nm, less_than_2pct, greater_than_5pct, switch_year) %>%
  distinct(customer_cd, master_customer_nm, less_than_2pct, greater_than_5pct, switch_year) %>%
  arrange(switch_year)

list2_5 <- switchers %>% select(customer_cd) %>% left_join(hospdetails, by = c(customer_cd = "Master Customer Cd")) %>% distinct()  

switchers_details <- switchers %>% 
  left_join(hospdetails, by = c(customer_cd = "Master Customer Cd")) |>
  select(-c("Master Customer Nm", less_than_2pct, greater_than_5pct, "Ship To Cntry")) |>
  rename("Hospital" = "master_customer_nm", 
       "Address" =  "Ship To Addr1"  ,
       "City"  =  "Ship To City"  ,
       "State" =  "Ship To State",
       "Zip"  =  "Ship To Zip"  ,
       "Cancer Ctr"  = "cancer_ctr_flg",
       "Trauma Ctr"=  "trauma_ctr_flg",
       "Bed Cnt"=  "Bed Cnt"      ,
       "Surgeries Cnt"= "Surgeries Cnt" ,
       "Switch Yr." = switch_year
           ) |>
  distinct()

# writexl::write_xlsx(x=list2_5, path ="E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\list2_5.xlsx")
# download_file( 
#   path = "E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\list2_5.xlsx",
#   output_name = "Hospitals_w_LTOWB_2to5",
#   button_label = "download data (Excel)" ,
#   button_type = "info",
#   has_icon = TRUE,
#   self_contained = TRUE
#   )


switchers_det <- switchers_details %>% rename(Beds = `Bed Cnt`, Surgeries = `Surgeries Cnt`) %>%
  select(-c(Address)) %>% 
    mutate( n_filled = rowSums(!is.na(.) & trimws(as.character(.)) != "") ) %>%
  group_by(customer_cd) %>%
  slice_max(n_filled, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-n_filled) %>% arrange(desc(`Beds`))
  
writexl::write_xlsx(x=switchers_det, path ="E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\switchers_details.xlsx")
download_file( 
  path = "E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\switchers_details.xlsx",
  output_name = "Details of Hospitals_w_LTOWB_2to5",
  button_label = "download hospital details" ,
  button_type = "info",
  has_icon = TRUE,
  self_contained = TRUE
  )

# switchers %>%
#   select(customer_cd, master_customer_nm, less_than_2pct, greater_than_5pct, switch_year) %>%
#   rename(
#     `<2% LTOWB` = less_than_2pct,
#     `>5% LTOWB` = greater_than_5pct
#   ) %>%
#   kable(
#     caption = "Hospitals Switching from <2% to >5% LTWOB Usage",
#     booktabs = TRUE
#   ) %>%
#   kable_styling(
#     font_size = 14,        # << REDUCE FONT SIZE
#     full_width = FALSE,
#     position = "center"
#   )


switchers_det %>% gt() %>% 
  tab_header(title = "Table 1. Hospitals with LTOWB% from 2% or less to 5% or greater",
             subtitle = "arranged in descending order of bed size") %>%
  cols_label(customer_cd = "Hospital Code") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Hospital Characrteristics", columns = c(`Cancer Ctr`,`Trauma Ctr`, `Beds`, `Surgeries`, `Switch Yr.`)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Hospital Location", columns = c(`Hospital`,`City`, `State`, `Zip`)) %>%
  cols_align("left", columns = everything()) %>%
  tab_options(table.border.left.color = "white",
              table.border.right.color = "white",
              table_body.hlines.color = "gray") %>%
  theme_gt_compact() %>%
  tab_options(data_row.padding = px(1), column_labels.padding = px(1)) %>%
  # tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#f3f5f0")), locations = cells_body(rows = cluster == "Low penetration")) %>%
  # tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#fffbbf")), locations = cells_body(rows = cluster == "Moderate penetration")) %>%
  # tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#ddf5bf")), locations = cells_body(rows = cluster == "High penetration")) %>%
  tab_footnote("") %>%
  cols_width(
    customer_cd  ~px(87),
    Hospital ~ px(280),
    City     ~ px(97),
    State    ~ px(30),
    Zip ~ px(40),
    `Cancer Ctr` ~ px(65),
    `Trauma Ctr`    ~ px(70),
    Beds    ~ px(40),
    Surgeries ~ px(90),
    `Switch Yr.` ~ px(60),
    # contact ~ px(100),
    everything() ~ px(5)
  ) |>
  tab_options(table.font.size = px(12)) |>
tab_options(
    table.width = pct(100)
  )
```

##### Trajectory by hospitals  
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
unlink("trelliscope_ltowb", recursive = TRUE)

remove(switcher_details)
switcher_details <- hosp %>%
  semi_join(switchers, by = "customer_cd") %>%
  arrange(customer_cd, calendar_yr) %>%
  select(customer_cd, master_customer_nm, calendar_yr, ltwobcat, ltwobp)

switcher_details <- switcher_details %>%
  mutate('H' = paste0(customer_cd, " — ", master_customer_nm))

# install.packages("trelliscopejs")
remotes::install_github("hafen/trelliscopejs")
library(trelliscopejs)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
# Create a plotting function for each hospital
# plot_ltowb <- function(df) {
#   ggplot(df, aes(x = calendar_yr, y = ltwobp, color = ltwobcat)) +
#     geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
#     geom_point(size = 2) +
#     scale_x_continuous(breaks = df$calendar_yr) +
#     scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
#     scale_color_manual(values = c("<2.5" = "gray20",
#                                   "2.5-5.0" = "gray50",
#                                   ">5" = "gray80")) +
#     labs(
#       title = unique(df$customer_cd),
#       x = "Year",
#       y = "LTOWB (%)",
#       color = "Category"
#     ) +
#     theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
#     theme(legend.position = "bottom")
# }

# 2: Proper trelliscope call
# switcher_details %>%
#   group_by(customer_cd) %>%
#   nest() %>%
#   trelliscopejs::trelliscope(
#     name = "LTOWB Trends by Hospital",
#     path = "trelliscope_ltowb",
#     panelFn = function(x) plot_ltowb(x$data),
#     cogFn = function(x) {
#       tibble(
#         first_year = min(x$data$calendar_yr),
#         last_year  = max(x$data$calendar_yr),
#         max_ltwobp = max(x$data$ltwobp, na.rm = TRUE)
#       )
#     },
#     nrow = 2,
#     ncol = 2
#   )


# Plot LTOWB% vs Year using facet_trelliscope
library(trelliscopejs)
library(scales)

switcher_details %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = calendar_yr, y = ltwobp, color = ltwobcat)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(switcher_details$calendar_yr))) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("<2"="gray20","2-5"="gray50",">5"="gray80")) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "LTOWB (%)", color = "Category") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  facet_trelliscope(
    ~ H,
    nrow = 1,
    ncol = 2,
    path = "trelliscope_ltowb"
  )
```

- **Hospital‑Level Trends in Low‑Titer Whole Blood (LTWOB) Use, 2018–2025**

##### Hospital Counts: <2% to >5% LTOWB use  
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
switch_year_counts <- switchers %>%
  count(switch_year, name = "n_hospitals_switched")

n = max(switch_year_counts$n_hospitals_switched) + 2
ggplot(switch_year_counts,
       aes(x = switch_year, y = n_hospitals_switched)) +
  geom_col(fill = "gray40") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_hospitals_switched),
            vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Count of Hospitals Increasing LTOWB usage from <2% to >5% by Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of switching hospitals"
  ) + scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(), limits = c(0, n)) +
  theme_pubclean(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14) )

```

- Across the study period, several hospitals transitioned from low LTWOB utilization 
(<2%) to high utilization (>5%). 
- These transitions occurred predominantly after 2023, 
consistent with the system‑level increase in hospitals adopting higher LTWOB usage in later years 


#### Hospitals without increase in LTOWB use{.tabset #csp-down}
##### <2% hospital list
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
less_than_2perc <- hosp %>%
  group_by(customer_cd) %>%
  summarise(
    max_ltwobp = max(ltwobp, na.rm = TRUE),
    always_less_2 = max_ltwobp < 2,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(always_less_2)
less_than_2perc_details <- hosp %>%
  semi_join(hosp, by = "customer_cd") %>%
  distinct(customer_cd, master_customer_nm) %>%
  arrange(customer_cd)

list2 <- less_than_2perc_details %>% select(customer_cd) %>% 
  left_join(hospdetails, by = c(customer_cd = "Master Customer Cd"))   %>% 
  select(-c("Ship To Cntry", "Ship To Addr1")) |> distinct() %>%
  rename("Hospital" = "Master Customer Nm", 
       "City"  =  "Ship To City"  ,
       "State" =  "Ship To State",
       "Zip"  =  "Ship To Zip"  ,
       "Cancer Ctr"  = "cancer_ctr_flg",
       "Trauma Ctr"=  "trauma_ctr_flg",
       "Beds"=  "Bed Cnt"      ,
       "Surgeries"= "Surgeries Cnt" 
           ) |>
  distinct() %>% filter(City != '') %>% distinct() %>% #distinct(customer_cd, Hospital, City, State, `Cancer Ctr`,`Trauma Ctr`, `Beds`, `Surgeries`)
  arrange(desc(`Beds`))

# list2 <- less_than_2perc_details %>% select(customer_cd) %>% 
#   left_join(hospdetails, by = c(customer_cd = "Master Customer Cd")) %>% distinct()  

# list2_details <- list2 %>% 
#   left_join(hospdetails, by = c(customer_cd = "Master Customer Cd")) |>
#   select(-c("Master Customer Nm", "Ship To Cntry")) 
#   rename("Hospital" = "master_customer_nm", 
#        "Address" =  "Ship To Addr1"  ,
#        "City"  =  "Ship To City"  ,
#        "State" =  "Ship To State",
#        "Zip"  =  "Ship To Zip"  ,
#        "Cancer Ctr"  = "cancer_ctr_flg",
#        "Trauma Ctr"=  "trauma_ctr_flg",
#        "Beds"=  "Bed Cnt"      ,
#        "Surgeries"= "Surgeries Cnt" 
#            ) |>
#   distinct()


list3 <- list2 %>%
  mutate(
    n_filled = rowSums(!is.na(.) & trimws(as.character(.)) != "")
  ) %>%
  group_by(customer_cd) %>%
  slice_max(n_filled, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-n_filled) %>% arrange(desc(Beds))

writexl::write_xlsx(x=list3, path ="E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\list3.xlsx")
download_file( 
  path = "E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\list3.xlsx",
  output_name = "Hospitals_w_LTOWB_2orbelow",
  button_label = "download data (Excel)" ,
  button_type = "info",
  has_icon = TRUE,
  self_contained = TRUE
  )

list3 %>% gt() %>% 
  tab_header(title = "Table 2. Hospitals with LTOWB% below 2%",
             subtitle = "arranged in descending order of bed size") %>%
  cols_label(customer_cd = "Hospital Code") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Hospital Details", columns = c(`Cancer Ctr`,`Trauma Ctr`, `Beds`, `Surgeries`)) %>%
  cols_align("left", columns = everything()) %>%
  tab_options(table.border.left.color = "white",
              table.border.right.color = "white",
              table_body.hlines.color = "gray") %>%
  theme_gt_compact() %>%
  tab_options(data_row.padding = px(1), column_labels.padding = px(1)) %>%
  # tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#f3f5f0")), locations = cells_body(rows = cluster == "Low penetration")) %>%
  # tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#fffbbf")), locations = cells_body(rows = cluster == "Moderate penetration")) %>%
  # tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#ddf5bf")), locations = cells_body(rows = cluster == "High penetration")) %>%
  tab_footnote("") %>%
  cols_width(
    customer_cd  ~px(87),
    Hospital ~ px(280),
    City     ~ px(97),
    State    ~ px(30),
    Zip ~ px(40),
    `Cancer Ctr` ~ px(65),
    `Trauma Ctr`    ~ px(70),
    Beds    ~ px(40),
    Surgeries ~ px(90),
    # contact ~ px(100),
    everything() ~ px(5)
  ) |>
  tab_options(table.font.size = px(11)) |>
tab_options(
    table.width = pct(100)
  )
```


##### <2% hospital trajectory
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
less_than_2perc_long <- hosp %>%
  semi_join(less_than_2perc, by = "customer_cd") %>%
  arrange(customer_cd, calendar_yr) %>%
  select(customer_cd, master_customer_nm, calendar_yr, ltwobp, ltwobcat)

library(trelliscopejs)

less_than_2perc_long %>%
  mutate(panel_label = paste0(customer_cd, " — ", master_customer_nm)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(calendar_yr, ltwobp)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal() +
  facet_trelliscope(~ panel_label,
                    nrow = 2, ncol = 3,
                    path = "trelliscope_low_ltowb")

```


### Median PLT:RBC by LTWOB%
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# Summarize: mean and SE (cluster-robust CIs optional in B)
summary_by_year <- df %>%
  group_by(ltwobcat, calendar_yr) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_pltrbc = median(pltrbcratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_pltrbc = sd(pltrbcratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_pltrbc = sd_pltrbc / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_l = median_pltrbc - qt(0.975, pmax(n-1,1)) * se_pltrbc,
    ci_u = median_pltrbc + qt(0.975, pmax(n-1,1)) * se_pltrbc
  )

# Line with points and ribbons by category
ggplot(summary_by_year,
       aes(x = calendar_yr, y = median_pltrbc, color = ltwobcat, group = ltwobcat)) +
  # geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_l, ymax = ci_u, fill = ltwobcat),
  #             color = NA, alpha = 0.12, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(summary_by_year$calendar_yr))) +
  labs(
    x = "Calendar year",
    y = "PLT:RBC ratio",
    color = "LTWOB %usage",
    title = "Figure 1. Change in PLT:RBC ratio by LTWOB category over time (descriptive medians)"
  ) +
  theme_pubclean(base_size = 12) +
  ylim(0,1.2)
```

- Across eight years of observation, only hospitals with >5% LTWOB usage demonstrated a substantial temporary rise in PLT:RBC ratios, peaking in 2020, with subsequent decline and convergence toward the lower utilization categories by 2023–2025.
- These descriptive patterns suggest early implementation‑related variability in high‑LTWOB hospitals, with stabilization of transfusion ratios over time.
  + *Note: These values represent descriptive medians and do not reflect model‑adjusted estimates or tests of statistical significance.*


### PLT:RBC by year by LTOWB use{.tabset}
#### Mixed model: Random intercept for hospital
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
library(pbkrtest)
hosp <- hosp %>%
  mutate(
    year_f = factor(calendar_yr),              # like calendar_yr_c in SAS
    customer_cd = factor(customer_cd)
  )

#Fit linear mixed model (Gaussian, identity link; REML ~ PROC MIXED default)
m <- lmer(pltrbcratio ~ ltwobcat * year_f + (1 | customer_cd), data = hosp, REML = TRUE)

#--- Global Type III tests (focus on the interaction)
a <- anova(m, type = 3, ddf = "Kenward-Roger") #If pbkrtest is not installed, lmerTest will fall back to Satterthwaite

# Replace row names with publication‑ready labels
rownames(a) <- c("LTWOB category",
                 "Calendar year",
                 "LTWOB category × year")

a
```

- A linear mixed model (hospital random intercept) showed a highly significant 
**LTWOB category × year** interaction (p < 1.3e-33), 
  + indicating that differences in PLT:RBC ratio across LTWOB adoption levels 
    changed substantially over time.
- Main effects for **year** (p < 10⁻⁹) and **LTWOB** category (p = 0.019) were also significant, 
but are not interpreted because of the strong interaction.

#### Within-year tests
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# ======================================================
# 4) Within-year tests: Do LTWOB categories differ *in each year*?
#    Tukey-adjusted pairwise differences and overall F per year
# ======================================================
emm <- emmeans(m, ~ ltwobcat | year_f)   # LS-means by category within each year
# Tukey-adjusted pairwise comparisons within each year
pw <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")

# Convert to clean data frame
pw_df <- as.data.frame(pw)

library(knitr)
remotes::install_github("haozhu233/kableExtra")
library(kableExtra)
# Clean and format the output
pw_clean <- pw_df %>%
  mutate(
    Year     = as.character(year_f),
    Contrast = contrast,
    Estimate = ifelse(is.na(estimate), "nonEst", sprintf("%.3f", estimate)),
    SE       = ifelse(is.na(SE),       "NA",     sprintf("%.3f", SE)),
    DF       = ifelse(is.na(df),       "NA",     sprintf("%.0f", df)),
    t_ratio  = ifelse(is.na(t.ratio),  "NA",     sprintf("%.2f", t.ratio)),
    p_value  = ifelse(
                  is.na(p.value), 
                  "NA",
                  ifelse(p.value < 0.0001,
                         "<0.0001",
                         sprintf("%.4f", p.value)))
  ) %>%
  select(Year, Contrast, Estimate, SE, DF, t_ratio, p_value)

# Render as formatted publication-style table
pw_clean %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Tukey-adjusted pairwise differences in PLT:RBC ratio by LTWOB category within each calendar year.",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")

```

- The output shows within‑year comparisons among the 3 LTWOB categories:
  + '<2% LTOWB use'
  + '2.5–5.0% LTOWB use'
  + '>5% LTOWB use'
 
- These are LS‑mean (model‑adjusted) differences in PLT:RBC ratio, computed separately for each year.
  + The Tukey method adjusts for the 3 comparisons within each year.
  
- **2018**: No estimable comparisons.
- **2019**: No differences.
- **2020**: highly significant spike in the >5 category vs both other groups.
  + <2 and 2.5–5.0 do not differ (p ≈ 0.99).
  + Both are dramatically lower than >5 (difference ≈ −3.0 units, p < 0.0001).
  + **Conclusion (2020)**: The hospitals with >5% LTOWB usage have significantly higher PLT:RBC than both other LTWOB groups.
- **2021**: No differences.
- **2022**: *Reverse effect*: >5 category now has significantly lower PLT:RBC ratio than both 2.5–5.0 and <2.
<2 and 2.5–5.0 do not differ significantly. Both have significantly higher PLT:RBC ratio than the >5 category.
**Conclusion:** A reversal compared to 2020. Now hospitals with >5% LTOWB usage have significantly lower PLT:RBC ratio.
- **2023–2025**: No meaningful differences; groups converge in 2023; LTWOB categories remain similar 

### Model-adjusted means 
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
emm_df <- as.data.frame(emm) %>%
  rename(emmean = emmean, lower = lower.CL, upper = upper.CL) %>%
  mutate(calendar_yr = as.integer(as.character(year_f)))

ggplot(emm_df, aes(calendar_yr, emmean, color = ltwobcat, group = ltwobcat)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("<2"="#e15759","2-5"="#59a14f",">5"="#4e79a7"),
                     name = "LTWOB category") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(emm_df$calendar_yr))) +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 2. Adjusted PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals categorized by LTWOB % over time (mixed model LS-means)",
    x = "Calendar year", y = "Adjusted PLT:RBC ratio (LS-mean)"
  ) +
  theme_pubclean(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```


- **Comparision with the Raw Descriptive Medians (Unadjusted) in Figure 1**
- Figure 1 displays simple medians of the observed PLT:RBC ratio within each LTWOB category and year, without adjusting for:
  + differences in hospital size
  + differences in case mix
  + differences in hospital‑level random effects (baseline transfusion tendency)
  + imbalance in the number of hospitals per category
  + changes in category membership over time
- Because these medians reflect the observed distribution only, they are susceptible to:
  + influence of a few hospitals with unusually high ratios in that year
  + differences in which hospitals fall into each LTWOB category each year
  + uneven sample sizes across categories
  + regression dilution or skewed distributions
- Thus, Figure 1 is based on the data as it is, before accounting for clustering and model structure.

- **Figure 2** above is the Model‑Adjusted LS‑means (Mixed Model)
  + displays the least‑squares means from a linear mixed‑effects model with:
    * hospital random intercepts (accounting for repeated measures and baseline differences),
    * a LTWOB category × year interaction,
    * and adjustment for the unbalanced structure of the data.

- Therefore, the LS‑means represent the expected PLT:RBC ratio for each category and year after controlling for hospital‑level variability.
- This modeling makes important changes:
  + Removes influence of hospitals with unusually high or low raw values
    * 2022 may contain hospitals with extreme ratios in certain LTWOB categories.
    * Random effects shrink those extremes toward the group mean.
  + Adjusts for differences in the number of hospitals per category
    * If 2022 has fewer hospitals in the >5% category, the LS‑mean is estimated with more shrinkage and may differ from the raw median.
- Corrects for category switching
  + If some hospitals moved between LTWOB categories from 2020 → 2022, the unadjusted medians reflect this churn, but the mixed model handles repeated measures appropriately.

- The >5% category shows a markedly lower adjusted mean in 2022, dropping below both <2% and 2.5–5.0%.
- The 2.5–5.0% group appears elevated relative to others, with wide confidence intervals.
- In 2022, the mixed model identifies hospital‑level heterogeneity that the raw medians cannot correct for:
  + Hospitals in the >5% category may have negative random effects (lower baselines), pulling their LS‑mean down.
  + Hospitals in the 2.5–5% category may have positive random effects or greater stability, elevating their LS‑mean.
  +Observed medians in 2022 may be influenced by 1–2 hospitals with extreme values; the model reduces their influence.

**Thus, the unadjusted Figure 1 reflects what was observed in 2022, while the adjusted Figure 2 reflects the estimated values after accounting for differences between hospitals, repeated measures, and unbalanced categories.**


### Effect sizes{.tabset}
#### Effect sizes as differences vs a reference category by year{.tabset}
##### ">5 minus <2"
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
ref_contr <- contrast(emm, method = list(">5 minus <2" = c(-1, 0, 1)), adjust = "none")
res <- summary(ref_contr, infer = TRUE)

res_clean <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Year = year_f,
    Contrast = contrast,
    Estimate = estimate,
    Lower.CL = lower.CL,
    Upper.CL = upper.CL,
    P.value = p.value
  )

res_clean <- res_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Estimate = ifelse(is.na(Estimate), "nonEst", sprintf("%.3f", Estimate)),
    Lower.CL = ifelse(is.na(Lower.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Lower.CL)),
    Upper.CL = ifelse(is.na(Upper.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Upper.CL)),
    P.value  = ifelse(
                 is.na(P.value), "NA",
                 ifelse(P.value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", P.value))
               )
  )

res_clean %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Adjusted Difference (>5% minus 2.5–5%) in PLT:RBC Ratio by Year",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```


- These estimates represent adjusted mean differences in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% LTWOB 
usage and those with <2%, generated from a linear mixed model including a hospital‑level random intercept 
and a LTWOB category × year interaction. 
  + Positive values indicate higher PLT:RBC ratios in the >5% group; 
  + Negative values indicate lower ratios. 
  + Confidence intervals are 95%, with Kenward–Roger degrees of freedom.
  + The adjusted difference in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% versus <2% 
  + LTWOB utilization varied substantially over time. 

- A pronounced elevation in the >5% group was observed in 2020 (difference, 3.03; p < 0.0001), representing a strong 
early divergence in component use.
- By 2022, this pattern reversed, with the >5% group demonstrating significantly lower adjusted ratios 
(difference, −0.90; p = 0.012).
- No significant differences were observed in 2019 or 2021, nor in 2023–2025, indicating a return to alignment 
across LTWOB categories in later years.
- The adjusted difference in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% and <2 LTWOB utilization varied 
substantially over time (significant interaction). A marked elevation in PLT:RBC ratio was observed 
among >5% hospitals in 2020, with adjusted differences exceeding 3 units compared with the <2% group (p < 0.0001).
- By 2022, this pattern reversed, with the >5% group demonstrating significantly lower adjusted ratios 
(difference ≈ −0.9; p = 0.01).
- For all subsequent years (2023–2025), the categories showed no meaningful differences, with point estimates clustered near zero.

##### ">5 minus 2.5-5"
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
ref_contr <- contrast(emm, method = list(">5 minus 2.5-5" = c(0, -1, 1)), adjust = "none")

res <- summary(ref_contr, infer = TRUE)

res_clean <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Year = year_f,
    Contrast = contrast,
    Estimate = estimate,
    Lower.CL = lower.CL,
    Upper.CL = upper.CL,
    P.value = p.value
  )

res_clean <- res_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Estimate = ifelse(is.na(Estimate), "nonEst", sprintf("%.3f", Estimate)),
    Lower.CL = ifelse(is.na(Lower.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Lower.CL)),
    Upper.CL = ifelse(is.na(Upper.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Upper.CL)),
    P.value  = ifelse(
                 is.na(P.value), "NA",
                 ifelse(P.value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", P.value))
               )
  )

res_clean %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Adjusted Difference (>5% minus 2.5–5%) in PLT:RBC Ratio by Year",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

- The adjusted difference in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% and 2.5–5% LTWOB utilization varied 
substantially over time (significant interaction). A marked elevation in PLT:RBC ratio was observed 
among >5% hospitals in 2020, with adjusted differences exceeding 3 units compared with the 2.5–5% group (p < 0.0001).
- A pronounced elevation in the >5% group was observed in 2020 (difference, 3.06; p < 0.0001), representing a strong 
early divergence in component use.
- By 2022, this pattern reversed, with the >5% group demonstrating significantly lower adjusted ratios 
(difference, −1.47; p = 0.003).
- No significant differences were observed in 2019 or 2021, nor in 2023–2025, indicating a return to alignment 
across LTWOB categories in later years.
- For all subsequent years (2023–2025), the categories showed no meaningful differences, with point estimates clustered near zero.
> Temporal fluctuations in PLT:RBC ratio among hospitals with higher LTWOB usage suggest early variability in transfusion 
practices during whole‑blood program rollout, followed by stabilization and convergence across hospitals in subsequent years.
